core_mechanism: an absorbing-state (contact-process / directed-percolation-type) non-equilibrium phase transition — a competition between a spreading/proliferation process and an extinction/recovery process producing a critical threshold — whose universality class is continuously tuned by a discretization/interpolation parameter, with interference between paths shifting it from one class to another
brief_reason: The abstract describes competition between a coherent infection (spreading) process and a dissipative recovery (extinction) process that undergoes a phase transition, whose universality class is continuously interpolated by the degree of discretization (Trotterization) via destructive interference between contributing paths.
